verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain (confirmed by multiple sources) and is predicted to be a soluble protein. It localizes to the intra-acrosomal compartment of sperm — an internal vesicular space — rather than the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. Protein Atlas shows cytoplasmic expression in seminiferous duct cells. NCBI places it in the nucleus. Checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/oncogenic, cell-death, activation-induced, or stress-related outer-leaflet ecto-pool; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — while expression is testis/sperm-restricted, IZUMO4 is intra-acrosomal (internal vesicular lumen), not anchored to or displayed on the outer leaflet of sperm plasma membrane — intra-acrosomal ≠ surface-accessible; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — IZUMO4 is not a lysosomal TM protein; it is a soluble intra-acrosomal protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented minority PM pool; loss after acrosome reaction indicates it is released/lost, not surface-displayed; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no evidence of wash-resistant covalent or non-covalent tethering to a TM partner on the outer leaflet. No surface-directed therapeutic programs identified. The protein has no TM domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation.
